title: Two-Agent Router
constraints: You must use exactly two specialized agents plus a router
The router must classify intent before delegating
Each agent must only handle its own domain
Do not combine billing and tech support logic in one agent
hints: Classify the user's intent as either billing or tech support before routing
Create separate agents with focused system prompts for each domain
Use a triage agent or function that inspects the query and hands off to the correct specialist

starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: A single agent tries to handle both billing and tech support
# TODO: Create separate billing and tech-support agents with a router
agent = Agent(
    name="Customer Support",
    instructions="You are a customer support agent. Handle all billing questions and technical issues.",
)

# Test with billing query
result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "I was charged twice for my subscription last month")
print(result.final_output)

# Test with tech support query
result = Runner.run_sync(agent, "My app keeps crashing when I try to export PDFs")
print(result.final_output)
starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: A single chain handles both billing and tech support
# TODO: Create separate billing and tech-support chains with a router
prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.from_messages([
    ("system", "You are a customer support agent. Handle all billing questions and technical issues."),
    ("human", "{input}"),
])

chain = prompt | llm

# Test with billing query
result = chain.invoke({"input": "I was charged twice for my subscription last month"})
print(result.content)

# Test with tech support query
result = chain.invoke({"input": "My app keeps crashing when I try to export PDFs"})
print(result.content)
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process

# BUG: A single agent tries to handle both billing and tech support
# TODO: Create separate billing and tech-support agents with a router
support_agent = Agent(
    role="Customer Support",
    goal="Handle all customer support inquiries including billing and technical issues.",
    backstory="You are a customer support agent. Handle all billing questions and technical issues.",
)

# Test with billing query
task1 = Task(
    description="I was charged twice for my subscription last month",
    expected_output="A helpful response addressing the billing concern.",
    agent=support_agent,
)

crew = Crew(agents=[support_agent], tasks=[task1], process=Process.sequential)
result = crew.kickoff()
print(result)

# Test with tech support query
task2 = Task(
    description="My app keeps crashing when I try to export PDFs",
    expected_output="A helpful response addressing the technical issue.",
    agent=support_agent,
)

crew = Crew(agents=[support_agent], tasks=[task2], process=Process.sequential)
result = crew.kickoff()
print(result)

evaluationCriteria.label: Correct intent routing
evaluationCriteria.description: Billing queries go to the billing agent and tech queries go to the tech support agent

evaluationCriteria.label: Two specialized agents exist
evaluationCriteria.description: There are exactly two specialist agents with focused, non-overlapping instructions

evaluationCriteria.label: Domain isolation maintained
evaluationCriteria.description: Each agent only responds within its own domain and does not leak into the other
